title: The Calm & Safe Money Reset
subtitle: A ten-minute weekly ritual for putting down the survival energy around money.
excerpt: The first of Rebecca's twelve weekly money ceremonies: a slow practice for energetically setting down the survival tension that accumulates around money over the course of a week.
sourceNotes: From Week 1 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448). The first of the twelve weekly rituals that companion the MONEY tapping program. The Prepare / Release / Allow / Integrate / Anchor structure is Rebecca's own.

practice.whenToUse: Use at the end of a hard money week, or as a weekly Sunday-evening reset, to put down what's accumulated around money over the past seven days.

content.text: Money picks up tension over a week. The bills opened in a rush. The transfer that didn't go through. The conversation with the partner that ended with no resolution. By Sunday evening it has all settled into the body somewhere, and a fresh week starts on top of it.
This is a ten-minute weekly ritual for putting that down. Five parts: prepare, release, allow, integrate, anchor. Move through them slowly; the slower the better.
What you'll need
Ten minutes alone, somewhere quiet. A pen and a single line of paper for the anchor at the end. A candle if you have one; a glass of water if you don't. Nothing else.
The practice
Prepare

Sit somewhere quiet. Light the candle if you have one. Take a few slow breaths. Picture all your bills, your accounts, your money thoughts of the past week resting gently in front of you. Not the resolved version, the actual version. The stress as it actually was.
Release
Say the following three times, either out loud or in your mind. Slowly.

attrs.quote: I am ready to release all fear, stress, and survival energy around money. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.

content.text: Allow
Now the other direction. Three times. Slowly.

attrs.quote: I am ready to align with and allow calm, peace, and safety with money. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.

content.text: Integrate
One big slow breath in. All the way out again. Let any tension drop with the breath.

Close your eyes. Picture a soft warm light filling the body, head to foot. Slow. Not dramatic. Peace, calm, safety spreading through each part of you. Feel yourself held by the room you're sitting in, the floor under your feet, the air around you. Stay for as long as feels right. A minute is plenty.
Anchor
Open your eyes. Pick up the pen. Write this single sentence in your own handwriting:

attrs.quote: This week, I am grateful that I am learning to feel safe with money.

content.text: Say it out loud. Once. Feel it in your body. Put the paper somewhere you'll see it during the week.
Blow out the candle if you lit one. Drink the water if you didn't.
What you might notice

The release lines often land deeper than the allow lines on the first pass. That's fine; the body releases faster than it allows. Some weeks the anchor sentence feels true straight away; some weeks it feels aspirational. Both are useful. The sentence is a direction, not a claim.

Repeat the ritual each Sunday evening for a few weeks before judging whether it works for you. The first time often clears the loudest week of stress; the cumulative effect is what does the slower work.
Where this practice comes from
This is the first of the twelve weekly rituals from Rebecca's
The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow
(2025), which sits alongside the eighty-four-day tapping program in
MONEY

(2025). The five-part Prepare / Release / Allow / Integrate / Anchor structure is Rebecca's own framework for closing a week. The remaining eleven weekly rituals in the journal companion the rest of the twelve-week program, from ancestral release in Week 2 through to completion in Week 12.
